alerts.title: Iran attacks Kuwait airport; US retaliates with strikes on radar sites
alerts.summary: Iran launched multiple drones and missiles at Kuwait International Airport and US military positions on June 4-5, killing 1 and injuring 63 per incidents feed (The Guardian, CBS News). US forces conducted counter-strikes on Iranian radar installations. CCTV footage confirms Shahed-136 drone struck Terminal 1. Active kinetic escalation represents material breach of ceasefire negotiations with threat level at 9/10 CRITICAL.

alerts.title: Hezbollah collapses Israel-Lebanon ceasefire within 48 hours; launches sustained attacks
alerts.summary: Despite US-brokered June 3 ceasefire agreement, Hezbollah resumed extensive rocket and drone strikes on Kiryat Shmona, Nahariyya, Shlomi and Mediterranean positions per Telegram (ME Spectator, 50K-68K views). FPV drones targeted IDF command vehicles. Major escalation cycle shows ceasefire framework failure and new threat phase emerging in northern theater.

alerts.title: Iran damaged US air operations center in Qatar during initial phase of conflict
alerts.summary: Air & Space Forces Magazine reports Iran successfully struck US air operations center in Qatar early in the conflict per incidents feed. Development confirms Iranian capability to penetrate critical US military infrastructure in theater and represents significant operational success that aligns with escalation pattern.

alerts.title: Iran pressuring Houthis to attack Red Sea tankers; proxy coordination escalates
alerts.summary: South China Morning Post reports Iran directing Houthi missile and naval attacks on Red Sea shipping per incidents feed. Multiple coordinated naval incidents confirmed (Courthouse News, BBC, AP News) with 3 killed in recent attack. Houthis and Al-Shabaab reportedly coordinating to choke maritime routes, signaling proxy network activation for sustained economic disruption.

alerts.title: Iran nuclear enrichment at 60% U-235; breakout timeline 1-2 weeks estimated
alerts.summary: Global metrics report Iran maintaining 60% uranium enrichment with breakout time estimated at 1-2 weeks per IAEA reports (Reuters, JNS.org, The Hill). IAEA unable to inspect facilities damaged in war. Nuclear program relatively unchanged since conflict onset but breakout window remains critically compressed amid active hostilities and escalating US-Iran kinetic operations.

analysis_note: US-Iran direct kinetic exchange now active with ceasefire frameworks collapsing across Israel-Lebanon and Gaza; nuclear breakout window at 1-2 weeks with proxy escalation in Red Sea and Persian Gulf creating multi-front pressure on US assets and allies.
